note: Vidhura-jataka narrative tag: 'making greeting with the naga-king he said' -- passing mention.

claim: Naga-king possesses iddhi, splendour, strength, and a celestial palace (vimana).
note: Verse-question framing the naga-king's iddhi/juti/bala/vimana; asserts the powers exist.

claim: Naga-king's vimana is asked whether self-made or god-given -- rebirth-fruit framing.
note: Question about how the naga obtained his estate; karma-cause framing.
Narrative tag 'hearing that, the naga-king said'.

claim: Asks what vow/holy-life in a former birth ripened as the naga-king's powers.
note: Gloss: 'what was your vow, what brahmacariya, of which good conduct is this the fruit' -- karma-cause of naga state.
Narrative tag.

claim: The naga's realm and state were obtained by merit (punna); fruit of good deeds.
note: 'since by your merits you got this naga-realm... so practise dhamma' -- naga rebirth as karma-fruit.
Narrative tag.

claim: The naga-king listens to the bodhisatta's dhamma-teaching.
note: 'the naga-king having heard the Great Being's dhamma-talk' -- naga hears Dhamma.
Narrative tag: 'making praise of the naga-king he spoke the verse'.
Narrative tag.
Narrative tag: 'the naga-king was pleased'.
Verse addressing a naga-maiden (nagi) in dialogue; no ontological claim, passing vocative.

claim: Naga-maiden possesses iddhi, splendour, strength, and a vimana.
note: Same vimana-question verse addressed to the naga-maiden; asserts her powers/estate.

claim: Naga-maiden's vimana queried as self-made or god-given (rebirth-fruit).
note: Karma-cause framing of naga-maiden's estate.
Verse: 'just as naga Varuna asked... so too the naga-maiden asked' -- narrative recap, no claim.
Verse recap of the wise one satisfying the naga and naga-maiden; passing mention.
Verse: addressed Varuna the naga-king and naga-maiden; passing mention.
Verse vocative to naga (Varuna) offering his heart/flesh; narrative, no ontological claim.
Word-gloss on the verse addressing Varuna; narrative gloss, no claim about naga-nature.
Narrative tag 'the naga-king said'.
Narrative: Varuna naga-king gave Irandhati to Punnaka; plot event, no ontological claim.
Verse: Punnaka glad having won the naga-maiden Irandhati; passing mention.

claim: Dhatarattha's sons are the listener's sister's-sons, naga nephews -- naga kinship/lineage.
note: Identifies humans/nagas in a kin line ('your bhagineyya-nagas'); naga genealogy/birth.

claim: Varuna is a naga named Bhumindhara (earth-bearer); his niece Irandhati is a naga-maiden.
note: Names the naga-king and naga-maiden with descriptive epithet; identity/realm framing, light claim.

claim: Bhumindhara dwells in the naga-realm (nagabhavana); the naga-king kept the four-uposatha question and returned to nagabhavana.
note: Rich block: naga-realm dwelling + naga-king pleased at catupposathika question, went to nagabhavana; realm_habitat (also uposatha + hears_dhamma framing).
Jataka samodhana: Varuna naga-king was Sariputta -- identification of past birth, plot bookkeeping, no naga-ontology claim.

claim: Nagas and supannas are amanussa (non-human) beings who ask questions, distinct from humans.
note: Glosses 'amanussa-puccha' as questions of nagas/supannas etc.; classifies naga as non-human class.

claim: Powerful naga-kings make the animal realm not strictly a 'bad destiny' (duggati).
note: Standard commentarial formula: tiracchanayoni is apaya but not duggati because of mighty naga-kings; classification/plane status.

claim: The Buddha's pure fame resounds in the naga-realm and supanna-realm.
note: 'visuddhasaddo in nagabhavana and supannabhavana' -- asserts naga-realm as a place where fame spreads; realm_habitat.
Quoted Bhuridatta-jataka verse 'in which direction does the naga-king stand'; passing citation, no claim.

claim: Among animals, the sense-pleasures of nagas and supannas are 'superior' (panita); ranks naga within tiracchana.
note: Hierarchy of kama: nagas/supannas are the panita animals; classification/plane status.

claim: A being can fall away (vippalujjati) from the naga-supanna world -- treats it as a world/plane.
note: Lists nagasupanna-loka alongside deva/manussa worlds as a plane one departs; realm_habitat/classification.

claim: A wrong view can be 'enough for naga-king-hood' as its samparayika (next-world) fruit.
note: 'alam nagattaya va' = sufficient for becoming a naga-king; rebirth as naga as karmic/ditthi fruit; karma_cause.

claim: Animal-beings incl. naga-kings (Apalala, Culodara, etc.) were tamed by the Buddha and set in the refuges and precepts.
note: Purisadamma-sarathi gloss: naga-kings tamed, made venomless, established in saranas/silas; conversion = takes refuge/hears dhamma.

claim: Mighty nagas/supannas/petas are listed among the 'bad destinies' (duggati).
note: 'yamaraja-naga-supanna-peta-mahiddhika' counted under duggatiyo; classification of naga plane.

claim: A meditator with iddhi can create a naga-form (nagavanna) by supernormal power.
note: Iddhi-katha on creating a naga-shape via vikubbana-iddhi; power/shapeshift (here a yogi making a naga form).

claim: Naga-form means snake-shape (sappa-santhana).
note: Defines nagavanna = sappasanthana (serpent-shape); ontological/morphology gloss.

claim: Nagas and supannas have sky-travel and other iddhi born of kamma (kammavipakaja iddhi).
note: Asserts nagas' flight/powers are kamma-result iddhi; power + karma_cause; coded power.
Chaddanta naga-king simile for the yogi delighting only in Himavant; simile vehicle (and Chaddanta is the elephant-king sense).
Simile string: naga-king in supanna's mouth wanting escape; simile vehicle for the yogi's mind.

claim: Jealous nagas/supannas can magically conjure obstacles (mountains, trees) against a sky-traveling monk.
note: Iddhividha gloss: nagas etc. usuyanta mapenti -- create obstructions; asserts naga iddhi/power.

claim: Powerful naga-kings make the animal realm not a strict 'bad destiny' (duggati).
note: Same duggati/apaya formula; classification of naga plane status.

claim: Powerful naga-kings make the animal realm not a strict 'bad destiny' (duggati).
note: Same standard formula; classification.
Cross-reference citation tag '(Erakapatta-naga-king story)' anchoring a Dhp verse; bibliographic pointer, no claim.

claim: The naga-king Manikantha wears a jewel on his hood, rises from the river and takes deva-form / his own form to approach an ascetic.
note: Describes hood (phana), jewel, shapeshifting from naga to deva-vanna and back; takes_human_form + power.
Passing reference to Ala-naga-king's chief-queen / a water-channel at Cittalapabbata; legal example, no naga-ontology claim.

claim: Powerful naga-kings make the animal realm not a strict 'bad destiny' (duggati).
note: Same duggati formula; classification of naga plane.

claim: A naga-youth (nagamanavaka) is an animal-being (tiracchanagata) before whom formal acts are invalid.
note: Vinaya gloss classing naga-manavaka among tiracchanagata; classification/plane status (esp. ritual-capacity).
Nagas in list of beings the Buddha instructed (deva-manussa-naga-yakkha); passing enumeration, no specific naga claim.

claim: Intercourse with a naga-maiden (in naga-form) is a parajika offence -- bears on a naga taking female form.
note: Nagi-vatthu: naga-manavika in animal-form yields parajika; relates to naga female form / monastic boundary; coded ordination_bar (disciplinary status), note takes_human_form.

claim: Naga-kings bring tooth-sticks (naga-creeper) and goods up from the naga-realm (nagabhavana).
note: Describes naga-kings provisioning from nagabhavana daily; realm_habitat + naga agency.
Verse vocative 'mahanaga' (great naga/tusker) in a challenge verse; honorific/simile address, no naga-being claim.

claim: A naga-king honours Thera Majjhantika; the monk is declared mightier (mahiddhikataro) than the naga-king.
note: Naga-king sets a jewel throne, fans the thera; comparison of iddhi-might; asserts naga's power/agency (Asivisopama context).

claim: Majjhantika won over (pacified) the fierce naga in Kasmira-Gandhara and freed it from bondage.
note: Verse: 'having gladdened the wicked naga he freed it'; conversion of a naga; hears_dhamma/conversion.
Verse list of fine substances incl. 'naga-brought collyrium'; the naga is a passing source-modifier in a list, no claim.

claim: Mahinda came alone to tame the naga-kings (his sister's-sons/uncle nagas) and tamed them.
note: Naga-kings as kin needing taming; asserts naga agency/danger and conversion; coded power (dangerous beings tamed), note kinship+hears_dhamma.
Nagas in list (deva-naga-yakkha did wonder-honour at the stupa); passing enumeration, no specific claim.

claim: Sanghamitta in supanna-form scared naga-clans; the nagas took the Bodhi-tree to the naga-realm and honoured it 7 days with naga-kingship.
note: Naga-clans, nagabhavana, naga-rajja, fear of supanna; realm + agency; coded takes_human_form? -> better realm_habitat. Set realm.

claim: Naga-kings (Apalala, Culodara, etc.) among animal-beings were tamed by the Buddha and set in refuges and precepts.
note: Purisadamma-sarathi gloss; naga-kings tamed/converted, established in saranas/silas.

claim: A naga-king (or supanna-youth) can set up a shop in human form (manussarupena).
note: Vinaya gloss: naga-king displaying wares in human form; asserts naga can assume human shape.

claim: Contact with a naga-maiden (animal-being) incurs dukkata -- naga female counts among tiracchanagata.
note: Kayasamsagga gloss classing naga-manavika under tiracchanagata-vatthu; disciplinary classification of naga.

claim: Naga-circle protective charms (naga-mandala parittas) are permissible -- presupposes naga venom/danger handled by spells.
note: Glosses 'paritta' as yakkha/naga-mandala charms; asserts naga-related apotropaic practice; coded power (venom/charm context).

claim: The naga's tejas (fiery power) was overcome by Sagata's own tejas.
note: Bhaddavatika naga: 'with his own tejas (he overcame) the naga's tejas'; asserts naga's fiery/venomous power.

claim: A dwelling can be a kappiya-kuti even if given to nagas (or devas/brahmas), implying nagas have dwellings/recipients.
note: Lists nagas among devata/brahma as possible donees of a vimana; treats nagas as realm-dwelling recipients; light realm claim.

claim: By his own tejas the Buddha/Sagata overcame the naga's tejas (fiery power).
note: Uruvela fire-naga: 'tejasa tejam... overcame, destroyed'; asserts naga's fiery power.

claim: Mucalinda the naga-king, born near his tree, of great power, coiled 7 times round the Buddha and spread his hood.
note: Describes naga-king's birth-locale, mahanubhava, coils (bhoga) and hood (phana) sheltering the Buddha; power + realm.

claim: A view-attained person (sotapanna) cannot have intercourse with a naga-maiden or accept animals -- bounds on the noble one re nagas.
note: Kathavatthu Duggatikatha: ditthisampanna cannot do such acts incl. with a naga-maiden; soteriological boundary; coded magga_phala_ceiling (here re the human's attainment touching naga).

claim: Naga-kings (Apalala, Culodara, etc.) among animal-beings were tamed by the Buddha, made venomless, set in refuges and precepts.
note: Vism Buddhanussati purisadamma-sarathi gloss; naga-kings tamed/converted.

claim: As the naga-king Bhuridatta the Bodhisatta kept uposatha limbs even while tortured by a snake-charmer.
note: Vism Metta-bhavana: Bhuridatta naga-king adhitthaya uposathangani; bodhisatta-as-naga + uposatha; coded bodhisatta_as_naga.

claim: As the naga-king Campeyya the Bodhisatta, harassed by a snake-charmer, gave rise to no mental ill-will.
note: Vism: Campeyya naga-king; bodhisatta-as-naga exemplifying patience.

claim: Powerful naga-kings make the animal realm not a strict 'bad destiny' (duggati).
note: Vism Cutupapata: same apaya/duggati formula; classification of naga plane.

claim: Nagas could not be protected from (a snake-handler/their venom) without the monk Rakkhita's power -- presupposes naga danger.
note: Vism Abhinna: 'could not protect the naga-kings'; context of naga peril; coded power (danger/venom). Light claim.
Section title 'Taming of the naga Nandopananda'; heading only, no asserted proposition.

claim: The naga-king Nandopananda is a wrong-viewed being unconfident in the Triple Gem -- a candidate for conversion.
note: Vism: introduces naga-king with micchaditthi; sets up his religious status/conversion; coded hears_dhamma framing.

claim: Nandopananda naga-king resents monks passing above his realm to the Tavatimsa heaven.
note: Describes naga-king's location below the deva-realm and his wrong view; realm_habitat + status.

claim: Nandopananda naga-king coiled 7 times round Mt Sineru and covered it with his hood, making darkness.
note: Vism: naga-king's coils (bhoga) + hood (phana) blotting out Sineru; power/iddhi.

claim: Moggallana takes a giant naga-king form; the naga emits smoke and fire in a power-duel.
note: Naga-king padhumayi/pajjali (smoke, fire); asserts naga's fiery iddhi (and a monk assuming naga-form).

claim: The naga-king plots to swallow Moggallana and emits a nose-blast (nasavata) of breath/venom.
note: Naga-king's poison-breath; asserts naga's lethal power.

claim: Defeated, the naga-king takes a brahmin-youth form (manavaka-vanna) and goes for refuge to Moggallana/the Buddha.
note: Naga shifts to human form, takes refuge; takes_human_form + conversion/hears_dhamma.
Chaddanta naga-king simile for the yogi delighting only in Himavant; simile vehicle (Chaddanta = elephant-king sense).
Simile string: naga-king in supanna's mouth wanting to escape; simile vehicle for the yogi's mind.
Makkhali Gosala's cosmology list: '49 hundred naga-abodes (nagavasa)' among speculative enumerations; doctrinal list-item, not the Buddha's assertion about nagas.
Here naga = the (royal) elephant to be guarded amid the women; per gate treated serpent but context is mount/scenery; no naga-being claim.

claim: Mucalinda the naga-king came from his realm, coiled 7 times round the Buddha and spread his great hood to shelter him from cold/rain.
note: Udana/KN Mucalinda: naga-king's coils + hood sheltering the Buddha; power + realm + protective act.
Sutta-nipata verse on ascetic practices; 'naga' tokens here are nagghi/naga-fire-tending sandhi artefacts in 'nagihuttassupasevana'; no naga-being claim.
Verse mocking water-purification: lists nagas (crocodiles var.) among water-creatures who would go to heaven; rhetorical list, simile-context, no ontological claim.
Theri/Thera verse: thighs 'like a naga's coils' (nagabhogasadisopama); beauty simile, vehicle not claim.
Apadana: nagas in list of beings (manussa, deva, naga, garula) building a stupa; passing enumeration / devotional scenery.
Apadana verses; 'naga' tokens are elephant-gait epithet (nagavikkantagamano) + sandhi (punagamam) + 'sa-deva-asura-naga' list; no naga-being ontology claim.
Apadana stupa-building list with nagas/garulas; devotional scenery + elephant-epithet (varanago, nagova); no claim.
Gotami-parinibbana apadana: 'nagas, asuras, brahmas honoured her'; nagas in a worship list; passing mention, no claim.

claim: Buddha Narada subdued the great naga-king Mahadona, performing a miracle before gods and men.
note: Buddhavamsa: 'taming the naga-king Mahadona'; conversion/subduing of a naga; hears_dhamma/conversion.
Buddhavamsa relic-distribution: one tooth-relic is 'in Nagapura' (place name); citizen/place sense, no naga-being claim.

claim: The naga-king (asked what he preaches) commends khanti (patience) -- and elsewhere naga vs garula contrast.
note: Cariya/Jataka verse: 'how does the naga-king speak? -- patience'; attributes a virtue-stance to the naga-king; light claim. naga-danta token = ivory peg (elephant) is incidental.
Cariya verse: rescued a man seized 'by a fierce naga' in the Ganges; narrative incident, naga as antagonist, no ontological claim.
Niddesa list of vatas (observances): 'naga-vata' among hatthi/assa/yakkha-vata etc.; list-item naming a naga-cult, no naga-being claim.

claim: The Buddha's pure fame resounds in the naga-realm (nagabhavana) and supanna-realm etc.
note: Niddesa: visuddhasaddo in nagabhavana/supannabhavana; asserts naga-realm as a place. (purisanago = epithet of Buddha, polysemy.)
Niddesa list of vata-observers (nagavatika) + naga-danta (ivory pegs); list-items, no naga-being ontological claim.
Netti/Patthana: 'the Muni teaches deva-naga-yakkha in their various tongues'; nagas in a teaching-audience list; passing, no specific claim.

Parajika uddana + 'nagas' diving (naganogahana) mnemonic; nagana, nagi, naga-danta (ivory) tokens are list/mnemonic + ivory-peg; no ontological claim here (the naga-ordination story itself is elsewhere).
Dhammapada vatthu heading 'Erakapatta-naga-king story' attached to a verse; section label/pointer, no asserted proposition.
Mahasudassana: 84,000 'naga'-thousands coming morning/evening for attendance = the king's elephants (nāga=elephant); royal mounts/scenery, no naga-being claim.
Sakkapanha: nagas in list 'deva, manussa, asura, naga, gandhabba' bound by jealousy; passing enumeration; the claim is about fetters generally, not naga-nature.
Lakkhana verse: gandhabba-naga among beings honouring the great man; passing devotional list (anagariya token is sandhi non-lexical).
Jataka uddana mnemonic listing 'Pandara-naga-king' as a tale-title; table-of-contents reference, no claim.

claim: A naga-king is ranked the foremost (settho) among nagas, set above by many honours -- naga status hierarchy.
note: Culahamsa verse: 'I cannot speak across the naga-king... he is our chief'; asserts naga-king's rank/status within naga-kind.

claim: A view-attained person (sotapanna) cannot have intercourse with a naga-maiden -- bounds on the noble one re nagas.
note: Kathavatthu Duggatikatha (Pali of the Abh §159 row); soteriological boundary touching naga-maiden; magga_phala_ceiling.
Balapandita: 'the elephant-treasure named Uposatha, the naga-king' = cakkavatti's elephant-jewel (nāga=elephant); regalia description, no naga-being claim.
Sandaka = Makkhali's cosmology list '49 hundred naga-abodes'; speculative enumeration reported as wrong-view, not an assertion about nagas.
Mahaniddesa vata-list: naga-vata among observances; list-item, no naga-being claim.

claim: The Buddha's pure fame resounds in the naga-realm (nagabhavana) and other realms.
note: Mahaniddesa parallel: visuddhasaddo in nagabhavana etc.; asserts naga-realm as a place. (purisanago = Buddha epithet, polysemy.)
Mahaniddesa vata-observer list (nagavatika) + naga-danta (ivory); list-items, no naga-being claim.
Parajika 4 uddana mnemonic with 'nagas diving' (naganogahana); mnemonic verse, no ontological claim.

claim: Nagas are great beings (bhuta) dwelling in the great ocean, some hundreds of yojanas in size.
note: Uposatha-khandhaka 'eight wonders of the ocean': great ocean is the abode of nagas etc., enormous attabhava; realm_habitat + size/classification.

claim: A naga in the Mandakini lotus-pond gives Moggallana lotus-fibres, ordering another naga to fetch them.
note: Bhesajja-khandhaka: nagas dwelling in/around a lotus-pond, with agency and hierarchy (one naga commands another); realm_habitat + agency.

claim: One with iddhi can abandon his natural form and display a naga-form (nagavanna).
note: Patisambhidamagga Iddhikatha: '...displays naga-vanna...'; here a meditator's shapeshifting, defining naga-form as a manifestable shape; power/morphology.
Mahaditthi = Makkhali's cosmology list '49 hundred naga-abodes'; wrong-view enumeration, not an assertion about nagas.
Khandhavagga uddana mnemonic listing 'naga' as a samyutta/vagga heading; table-of-contents, no claim.
Mara-samyutta uddana mnemonic listing 'naga' as a sutta-title; mnemonic, no claim.

Nagasutta: nagas grow body/strength relying on Himavant then descend to the sea -- extended simile for a bhikkhu cultivating the path; simile vehicle (the assertion is about the monk, not naga-ontology).
Nadisutta uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic, no claim.
Himavantasutta: same naga-Himavant simile for cultivating bojjhangas; simile vehicle, no naga-ontology claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.
Abbreviated vagga + uddana mnemonic listing 'naga'; mnemonic/peyyala, no claim.

claim: The naga-king Eravana, hearing the Buddha, came to him, conversed, and departed gladdened ('well said').
note: Dhammika-sutta: naga-king Eravana approaches the Buddha and hears dhamma; hears_dhamma.
Buddha-apadana: 'men, nagas, gandhabbas, all devas, come honour'; nagas in a devotional summons list; passing, no claim.
Apadana stupa-building: nagas in list of beings (manussa, deva, naga, garula); devotional scenery, no claim.

claim: In a past life the elder was the naga-king Varuna, ocean-dwelling, able to change form at will (kamarupi).
note: Moggallana-apadana: 'I was the naga-king Varuna... shapeshifting, ocean-dweller'; past-birth-as-naga + power + realm; coded bodhisatta_as_naga (here a disciple's own past, parallel category).
Sivali-apadana: 'gods, men, nagas, brahmas in assembly' as teaching-audience; passing enumeration, no naga claim.
Vangisa-apadana: Buddha honoured 'by devas, asuras, nagas, men'; passing devotional list, no claim.
Apadana stupa-building list with nagas/garulas; devotional scenery, no claim.
Gotami-parinibbana apadana: nagas/asuras/brahmas honour her (+ 'like a nagi' simile); worship list + simile, no ontological claim.
Apadana: nagas/kinnaras in list of beings born in various clans / future devotees; passing enumeration, no claim.
Punna-therigatha: mockery of water-rites listing nagas among water-creatures who'd be saved; rhetorical list, no ontological claim.
Ambapali-therigatha: thighs 'like a naga's coils' (nagabhogasadisopama); beauty simile, vehicle not claim.

claim: Mucalinda the naga-king came from his realm, coiled 7 times round the Buddha and spread his great hood to shelter him from cold/rain/insects.
note: Udana Mucalinda: naga-king's coils (bhoga) + hood (phana) protecting the Buddha; power + realm + protective act.
Abh-tika: defines 'naga-mandala' as a snake-charming ritual (make a circle, summon snakes, remove venom); glosses a human craft/charm, not an assertion about naga-beings.
Abh-tika: 'udaka' refers to Nagadipa, 'thala' to Jambudipa; Nagadipa is a place name (Sri Lankan locale); geography, no naga-being claim.
Cross-reference citation tag '(Erakapatta-naga-king story)' anchoring a Dhp-atthakatha quote; bibliographic pointer, no claim.
Cross-reference citation tag '(Erakapatta-naga-king story)'; bibliographic pointer, no claim.
Verse simile: '(fears X) as a naga-king (fears) a supanna'; the naga is a simile vehicle for fear, no ontological claim (though it presupposes naga-supanna enmity).

claim: The great enjoyments of nagas/supannas (even in the apaya) are the fruit of wholesome kamma, not unwholesome.
note: Abh-tika Kammacatukka: naga-supanna mahasampatti-vipakavinnana = fruit of kusala-kamma; karma_cause of naga fortune.

claim: Nagas/supannas have deva-like delightful enjoyments, golden colour, sweet voice -- all fruit of sense-sphere wholesome kamma.
note: Abh-tika Kamavacarakusala: naga-supanna bhoga/colour/voice are kamavacara-kusala phala; karma_cause of naga endowments.
